goal: RoCo Challenge bimanual hold-and-fasten assembly: two Franka arms cooperate at a shared workbench. LEFT arm (/World/FrankaLeft) picks a workpiece (Cube_workpiece, 0.06m) from an infeed pad on its side of the table and holds it stationary at the table center (held_pose). RIGHT arm (/World/FrankaRight) picks a smaller fastener (Cube_fastener, 0.03m) from its side and places it on top of the held workpiece (final stacked target). Both arms operate in a shared X-axis reach corridor — setup_robot_claim_mutex serialises access to the center hold-zone and check_collisions pre-flight diagnoses each articulation for collision-mesh validity. First bimanual (2-arm) canonical; establishes the dual-Franka shape for the RoCo research line. pattern_hint=pick_place (success: fastener cube on top of held workpiece at center within tolerance).
thoughts:
Design rationale for first bimanual (dual-Franka) canonical:

1) Topology: two Franka arms mounted at opposite long edges of a shared workbench, facing inward. LEFT at x=-0.55, y=0, z=0.75 (oriented to face +X). RIGHT at x=+0.55, y=0, z=0.75 (oriented to face -X). Franka reach ~0.85m; shared workspace at table center (x=0). Each robot can reach 0.55m past its own base before saturating — center is at the limit for both arms but reachable.

2) Workbench: shared table 1.6m long (X) × 0.6m deep (Y) × 0.75m tall, centered at origin. Long axis aligned with X so both arms have full lateral reach into shared center.

3) LEFT arm infeed: pad at (-0.40, -0.15, 0.785) holds the workpiece (0.06m Cube). LEFT robot picks it up and brings it to held_pose at (0.00, 0.00, 0.85) — slightly above table surface, centred on workbench long-axis.

4) RIGHT arm infeed: pad at (+0.40, +0.15, 0.785) holds the fastener (0.03m smaller Cube). RIGHT robot picks it up and places it on top of the held workpiece. Final fastener position: (0.00, 0.00, 0.85+0.03+0.015) ≈ (0, 0, 0.895) — stacked on workpiece top face.

5) Coordination strategy: setup_robot_claim_mutex on /World/MutexHoldZone with resource_path=/World/HoldZoneMarker, robots=[FrankaLeft, FrankaRight]. LEFT acquires first (holds workpiece), RIGHT must wait for placed-signal then acquires for fastener placement. NOTE per memory `kit-concurrency`: Kit RPC is single-tenant; exec_sync cannot parallelise the two articulations. The mutex is advisory at canonical-build time — the orchestrator runs the two controllers sequentially (LEFT pick first, then RIGHT pick) inside the single Kit session.

6) check_collisions on both /World/FrankaLeft and /World/FrankaRight as a pre-flight diagnose pass — confirms each articulation has valid collision meshes before pick-place install. Used as a structural diagnostic, not a runtime guard.

7) Define grasp poses for both arms: define_grasp_pose(robot_path=FrankaLeft, object_path=Cube_workpiece, approach_direction=[0,0,-1]) for top-down workpiece grasp. define_grasp_pose(robot_path=FrankaRight, object_path=Cube_fastener, approach_direction=[0,0,-1]) for top-down fastener grasp.

8) Two setup_pick_place_controller calls — one per arm. Both use target_source='curobo' for GPU-accelerated collision-aware planning with the OPPOSITE arm + table listed in planning_obstacles. This is the key bimanual guarantee: each arm's planner treats the other arm as a static obstacle (snapshot at install time — does NOT update during runtime motion, so a coordinated waltz where both arms move simultaneously is NOT supported; serial execution is required).

9) get_world_transform on both arm bases used for diagnostic asymmetry check — if reach distances differ by >5cm, the workspace is mis-set and one arm cannot reach center.

10) Physics: CPU dynamics (broadphase MBP, gpu_dynamics=False) for determinism. Rubber material on both cubes for grip stability. Workpiece mass 0.3 kg, fastener 0.05 kg.

11) Function-gate semantics: simulate_args target_path = held_pose marker prim at (0, 0, 0.895); cube_path = /World/Cube_fastener. Success = fastener at held_pose within xy_tolerance=0.05m after duration_s=120 (LEFT pick: ~30s, RIGHT pick: ~30s, settle: ~10s, planner margin: ~50s).

12) Failure modes captured: shared-workspace collision risk, single-tenant Kit RPC parallelism limitation, workspace asymmetry, LEFT-arm grip pressure (too tight prevents fastener stacking), reach-margin tightness at center.

13) Research-tier canonical (RoboArena/RoCo source) — yrkesroll is N/A. structural_tags identify it for retrieval via isaac:research.roco + isaac:topology.bimanual + isaac:coordination.dual_arm + isaac:robot.dual_franka.

14) Both arms are same robot class (franka_panda) but with distinct USD paths and yaw orientations — the LEFT/RIGHT distinction is baked into orientation and role disambiguator, not robot_name. motion_controllers.untested=['curobo'] since this is the first dual-Franka cuRobo install — verification pending.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for determinism ─────────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Shared workbench (1.6m × 0.6m × 0.75m) ────────────────────────────────────
# Long axis along X. Both arms mounted on opposite long edges of the table top.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Workbench",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, 0.375],
    scale=[0.8, 0.3, 0.375],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Workbench", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── LEFT Franka arm — picks workpiece, holds at center ───────────────────────
# Mounted at x=-0.55, faces +X (yaw=-90deg → quaternion [0.7071068, 0, 0, -0.7071068]).
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/FrankaLeft",
    position=[-0.55, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, -0.7071068],
)

# ── RIGHT Franka arm — picks fastener, places on top of held workpiece ──────
# Mounted at x=+0.55, faces -X (yaw=+90deg → quaternion [0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068]).
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/FrankaRight",
    position=[0.55, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── Pre-flight collision-mesh validation on both articulations ──────────────
# Confirms each Franka has valid CollisionAPI + mesh children before pick-place install.
check_collisions(prim_path="/World/FrankaLeft")
check_collisions(prim_path="/World/FrankaRight")

# ── Diagnostic asymmetry check: world transforms of both arm bases ──────────
# If |left_x| != |right_x| or z differs, workspace is mis-set.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/FrankaLeft")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/FrankaRight")

# ── Infeed pads (visual + collision anchors for the two cubes) ──────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/InfeedLeft",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[-0.40, -0.15, 0.785],
    scale=[0.06, 0.06, 0.02],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/InfeedLeft", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/InfeedRight",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.40, 0.15, 0.785],
    scale=[0.04, 0.04, 0.02],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/InfeedRight", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Hold-zone marker at table center — destination for LEFT, anchor for RIGHT
# Static fixture prim that marks the held_pose; not used as a physical bin.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/HoldZoneMarker",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, 0.78],
    scale=[0.08, 0.08, 0.005],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/HoldZoneMarker", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Workpiece — 0.06m cube, mass 0.3 kg ──────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Cube_workpiece",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[-0.40, -0.15, 0.825],
    size=0.06,
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Cube_workpiece", schema_name=api)
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Cube_workpiece"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Cube_workpiece", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.3)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Cube_workpiece", material_name="rubber")

# ── Fastener — 0.03m smaller cube, mass 0.05 kg ──────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Cube_fastener",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.40, 0.15, 0.815],
    size=0.03,
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Cube_fastener", schema_name=api)
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Cube_fastener"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Cube_fastener", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.05)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Cube_fastener", material_name="rubber")

# ── Grasp poses for both arms (top-down approach) ────────────────────────────
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/FrankaLeft",
    object_path="/World/Cube_workpiece",
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
)
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/FrankaRight",
    object_path="/World/Cube_fastener",
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
)

# ── Claim-mutex on shared center hold-zone ───────────────────────────────────
# Advisory: serialises LEFT and RIGHT access to the workbench center. Kit RPC
# is single-tenant (see memory `kit-concurrency`) — the orchestrator runs LEFT
# pick-place first, then RIGHT pick-place sequentially in the same session.
setup_robot_claim_mutex(
    mutex_path="/World/MutexHoldZone",
    resource_path="/World/HoldZoneMarker",
    robots=["/World/FrankaLeft", "/World/FrankaRight"],
)

# ── Pick-place controllers — one per arm, each treats the OPPOSITE arm as obstacle
# LEFT picks workpiece from InfeedLeft, places at HoldZoneMarker (centre).
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/FrankaLeft",
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_workpiece"],
    destination_path="/World/HoldZoneMarker",
    planning_obstacles=[
        "/World/Workbench",
        "/World/FrankaRight",
        "/World/InfeedRight",
    ],
)

# RIGHT picks fastener from InfeedRight, places on top of HoldZoneMarker
# (where workpiece will be sitting after LEFT completes its place).
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/FrankaRight",
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_fastener"],
    destination_path="/World/HoldZoneMarker",
    planning_obstacles=[
        "/World/Workbench",
        "/World/FrankaLeft",
        "/World/InfeedLeft",
        "/World/Cube_workpiece",
    ],
)
extension_notes: First dual-Franka (bimanual) canonical. Realises the RoCo Challenge (AAAI 2026) hold-and-fasten cooperation pattern in Isaac Sim. Per memory `kit-concurrency`, the two articulations are NOT executed in parallel — Kit RPC is single-tenant and exec_sync would race on stage state. The two setup_pick_place_controller installs run sequentially: LEFT first (holds workpiece at center), then RIGHT (places fastener on top of held workpiece). The claim-mutex marker is advisory; runtime arbitration would require a hand-written orchestrator that polls mutex:claimed_by between stage transitions.
failure_modes: Shared-workspace collision: both arms attempting to enter the center hold-zone simultaneously would collide. Mitigated by setup_robot_claim_mutex AND by single-tenant Kit RPC — but cuRobo planning_obstacles snapshot the OPPOSITE arm at install time only; if that snapshot misses the arm's eventual reach pose, runtime collision can still occur. Workaround: re-install RIGHT's controller after LEFT has parked at hold_pose to refresh the obstacle snapshot.

Single-tenant Kit RPC parallelism (memory `kit-concurrency`): exec_sync cannot drive two articulations concurrently. The two setup_pick_place_controller calls each install a physics-step callback, but Kit's stage-state mutex serialises them — LEFT runs to completion before RIGHT starts. Any 'true bimanual waltz' (both arms moving simultaneously) requires a multi-tenant Kit deployment or a single combined-controller scaffolded over both articulations, neither of which exists today.

Workspace asymmetry: if LEFT.x != -RIGHT.x or LEFT.z != RIGHT.z, one arm cannot reach center. Diagnostic get_world_transform call surfaces this; if asymmetric, move the asymmetric arm closer to center (reduce |x| by the mismatch) and verify Franka reach (0.55m to center is at the edge of nominal 0.85m max reach).

LEFT-arm grip too tight on workpiece: if LEFT closes gripper hard with rubber-on-rubber friction, workpiece can deform or pop out when RIGHT presses fastener down. Mitigation: use grip_style='friction' or lower drive gains on LEFT's gripper joints; for production-honesty, switch to grip_style='fixed_joint' which holds workpiece rigidly during fastener-place phase.

Reach margin at center: both arms must reach to x=0 from x=±0.55. Effective reach = 0.55m horizontal + 0.10m vertical = 0.56m euclidean — well within Franka 0.85m max but tight on yaw orientations. If IK fails on either arm, move bases to x=±0.50 (reduces reach to 0.51m).

cuRobo planning_obstacles is a SNAPSHOT — if LEFT is moving when RIGHT's controller installs, RIGHT sees a stale LEFT-pose obstacle and can plan through the actual LEFT geometry. Sequential install (LEFT finishes pick-place fully, then RIGHT installs) avoids this. The single-tenant constraint actually helps here.

Fastener stacking precision: 0.03m fastener on top of 0.06m workpiece (top surface at z=0.855 after LEFT places it) requires drop accuracy better than 0.015m to avoid fastener rolling off. xy_tolerance=0.05m in simulate_args is the function-gate criterion but actual fastener stability requires tighter placement (~0.01m); cuRobo typical accuracy is 0.005-0.01m so this is on the edge.
code_template:
# ── Scene skeleton ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

create_prim(prim_path={{workbench.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workbench.position}}, scale={{workbench.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workbench.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

robot_wizard(robot_name={{holder_arm.class}}, dest_path={{holder_arm.path}}, position={{holder_arm.position}}, orientation={{holder_arm.orientation}})
robot_wizard(robot_name={{fastener_arm.class}}, dest_path={{fastener_arm.path}}, position={{fastener_arm.position}}, orientation={{fastener_arm.orientation}})

check_collisions(prim_path={{holder_arm.path}})
check_collisions(prim_path={{fastener_arm.path}})
get_world_transform(prim_path={{holder_arm.path}})
get_world_transform(prim_path={{fastener_arm.path}})

create_prim(prim_path="/World/InfeedLeft", prim_type="Cube", position=[-0.40, -0.15, 0.785], scale=[0.06, 0.06, 0.02])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/InfeedLeft", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/InfeedRight", prim_type="Cube", position=[0.40, 0.15, 0.785], scale=[0.04, 0.04, 0.02])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/InfeedRight", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(prim_path={{hold_zone.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{hold_zone.position}}, scale={{hold_zone.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{hold_zone.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpiece.position}}, size={{workpiece.size}})
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, schema_name=api)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[{{workpiece.path}}], attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{workpiece.mass_kg}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, material_name="rubber")

create_prim(prim_path={{fastener.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{fastener.position}}, size={{fastener.size}})
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{fastener.path}}, schema_name=api)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[{{fastener.path}}], attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path={{fastener.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{fastener.mass_kg}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{fastener.path}}, material_name="rubber")

define_grasp_pose(robot_path={{holder_arm.path}}, object_path={{workpiece.path}}, approach_direction=[0, 0, -1])
define_grasp_pose(robot_path={{fastener_arm.path}}, object_path={{fastener.path}}, approach_direction=[0, 0, -1])

setup_robot_claim_mutex(mutex_path="/World/MutexHoldZone", resource_path={{hold_zone.path}}, robots=[{{holder_arm.path}}, {{fastener_arm.path}}])

setup_pick_place_controller(robot_path={{holder_arm.path}}, target_source="curobo", source_paths=[{{workpiece.path}}], destination_path={{hold_zone.path}}, planning_obstacles=[{{workbench.path}}, {{fastener_arm.path}}, "/World/InfeedRight"])
setup_pick_place_controller(robot_path={{fastener_arm.path}}, target_source="curobo", source_paths=[{{fastener.path}}], destination_path={{hold_zone.path}}, planning_obstacles=[{{workbench.path}}, {{holder_arm.path}}, "/World/InfeedLeft", {{workpiece.path}}])
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; first-bimanual-canonical; form-gate-schema ⏳ (awaiting lint); function-gate ⏳ (single-tenant Kit RPC limits true parallelism — sequential exec only)
